revision.text:
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]の[[智頭宿]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]で、[[江戸時代]]は[[因幡国]]藩主池田家の祈願所として栄えた。
境内は杉山の中腹に位置し、参道は[[智頭往来]]に接する。境内には古来より社紋である[[楓]]が植えられ数百本が繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。
==概要== *創立 - [[1278年]] *祭神 - 建御名方神 *神紋 - 楓葉 *例祭 - 春祭4/27、秋祭10/27 *鎮座地 - 〒689-1400 鳥取県八頭郡智頭町大字智頭宮前
==由緒== *1278年 - 信州諏訪神社の分霊を勧請奉祀し、諏訪大明神という名称で創建 *[[1583年]]12月 - [[羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失 *[[江戸時代]] - [[因幡国]]藩主池田家の祈願所となる *[[1782年]] - 御柱祭が創められる *[[1832年]]11月 - 本殿が再建される *[[1868年]]8月 - 合祭神を期に諏訪神社と改称す *[[1904年]]1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築
==御柱祭== 1782年に[[諏訪大社]]を倣い御柱祭が創められる。 以来6年おきの4月に柱献上者の山から[[杉]]が切り出され本殿四隅に建立される。
==交通アクセス== *[[智頭急行]]または[[JR]][[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]] 徒歩10分
{{stub}} [[Category:智頭町|すわしんしや]] [[category:鳥取県の神社|すわしんしや]]

'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]の[[智頭宿]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]で、[[江戸時代]]は[[因幡国]]藩主池田家の祈願所として栄えた。
杉山の中腹に鎮座し、参道は[[智頭往来]]に接する。境内には古来より社紋である[[楓]]が数百本が繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。
==概要== *創立 - [[1278年]] *祭神 - 建御名方神 *神紋 - 楓葉 *例祭 - 春祭4/27、秋祭10/27 *鎮座地 - 〒689-1400 鳥取県八頭郡智頭町大字智頭宮前
==由緒== *1278年 - 信州諏訪神社の分霊を勧請奉祀し、諏訪大明神という名称で創建 *[[1583年]]12月 - [[羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失 *[[江戸時代]] - [[因幡国]]藩主池田家の祈願所となる *[[1782年]] - 御柱祭が創められる *[[1832年]]11月 - 本殿が再建される *[[1868年]]8月 - 合祭神を期に諏訪神社と改称す *[[1904年]]1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築
==御柱祭== 1782年に[[諏訪大社]]を倣い御柱祭が創められる。 以来6年おきの4月に柱献上者の山から[[杉]]が切り出され本殿四隅に建立される。
==交通アクセス== *[[智頭急行]]または[[JR]][[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]] 徒歩10分
{{stub}} [[Category:智頭町|すわしんしや]] [[category:鳥取県の神社|すわしんしや]]

{{神社| |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[画像:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= [[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]大字智頭宮前 |位置= |祭神= 建御名方神 |社格= |創建= [[1278年]] |本殿= |別名= |例祭= [[4月27日]]、[[10月27日]] |神事= 御柱祭（6年おき） }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]の[[智頭宿]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]で、[[江戸時代]]は[[因幡国]]藩主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所として栄えた。 牛臥山の山麓に鎮座し、参道は[[智頭往来]]に接する。境内には古来より社紋である[[カエデ|楓]]が数百本繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。 [[画像:Chidu suwajinja04 1920.jpg|thumb|left|250px|拝殿前の楓の林]] {{-}} == 由緒 == [[画像:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|thumb|right|200px|石段]] * 1278年 - [[信濃国]][[諏訪大社]]の分霊を勧請奉祀し、「諏訪大明神」の社号で創建 * [[1583年]]12月 - [[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失 * 江戸時代 - 因幡国藩主池田家の祈願所となる * [[1782年]] - 御柱祭を開始 * [[1832年]]11月 - 本殿を再建 * [[1868年]]8月 - 合祭神を期に「諏訪神社」に改号 * [[1904年]]1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築 == 御柱祭 == 1782年に諏訪大社を倣い[[御柱祭]]が始められる。以来6年おきの4月に柱献上者の山から[[スギ|杉]]が切り出され本殿四隅に建立される。 == 交通アクセス == [[智頭急行]]またはJR[[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]]より徒歩10分 {{CommonscatN|Suwa-jinja, Chidzu, Tottori|諏訪神社}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社|すわしんしやちつ]] [[Category:智頭町]] [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[画像:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= [[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]大字智頭宮前 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} |祭神= 建御名方神 |社格= |創建= [[1278年]]（[[弘安]]元年） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= [[4月27日]]、[[10月27日]] |神事= 柱祭り（6年おき） }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]の[[智頭宿]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]で、[[江戸時代]]は[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所として栄えた。 牛臥山の山麓に鎮座し、参道は智頭往来に接する。境内には古来より社紋である[[カエデ|楓]]が数百本繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。 __TOC__ [[画像:Chidu suwajinja04 1920.jpg|thumb|left|250px|拝殿前の楓の林]] {{-}} == 由緒 == [[画像:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|thumb|right|200px|石段]] * [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]） - [[信濃国]][[諏訪大社]]の分霊を勧請奉祀し、「'''諏訪大明神'''」の社号で創建 * [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）12月 - [[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失 * 江戸時代 - 鳥取藩主池田家の祈願所となる * [[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]） - 柱祭りを開始 * [[天保]]3年（[[1832年]]）11月 - 本殿を再建 * [[慶応|慶應]]4年（[[1868年]]）8月 - 合祭神を期に「'''諏訪神社'''」に改号 * [[明治]]37年（[[1904年]]）1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築 == 柱祭り == 1782年（天明2年）より、諏訪大社の[[御柱祭]]に倣った「柱祭り」が行われている。以来6年ごとの[[申|申年]]と[[寅|寅年]]の4月に、柱を献上する者の山から4本の[[スギ|杉]]が伐り出され、町内を練り歩いた後、宮入りして本殿四隅に建立される。 2001年（平成13年）、鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財に指定。前回の開催は2004年（平成16年）で、次回は2010年（平成22年）4月に行われる。 == 交通アクセス == [[智頭急行]]またはJR[[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]]より徒歩15分 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja, Chidzu, Tottori|諏訪神社}} * [http://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/dd.aspx?menuid=1296 柱祭り]（智頭町役場ウェブサイト） {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社|すわしんしやちつ]] [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:智頭町]]

{{神社| |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[画像:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|250px]]

拝殿 |所在地= [[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]大字智頭宮前 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} |祭神= 建御名方神 |社格= |創建= [[1278年]]（[[弘安]]元年） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= [[4月27日]]、[[10月27日]] |神事= 柱祭り（6年おき） }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]の[[智頭宿]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]の[[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）で、[[信濃国|信州]]の[[諏訪大社]]の分霊を奉るため建てられた。[[江戸時代]]は[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所として栄えた。 [[画像:Suwazinzya1.jpg|thumb|left|180px|諏訪神社の紅葉]] 牛臥山の山麓に鎮座し、参道は智頭往来に接する。境内には古来より社紋である[[カエデ|楓]]が数百本繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。 __TOC__ [[画像:Chidu suwajinja04 1920.jpg|thumb|left|250px|拝殿前の楓の林]] {{-}} == 由緒 == [[画像:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|thumb|right|200px|石段]] [[画像:Suwazinzya2.jpg|thumb|right|200px|石段下の紅葉]] * [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]） - [[信濃国]][[諏訪大社]]の分霊を勧請奉祀し、「'''諏訪大明神'''」の社号で創建 * [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）12月 - [[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失 * 江戸時代 - 鳥取藩主池田家の祈願所となる * [[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]） - 柱祭りを開始 * [[天保]]3年（[[1832年]]）11月 - 本殿を再建 * [[慶応|慶應]]4年（[[1868年]]）8月 - 合祭神を期に「'''諏訪神社'''」に改号 * [[明治]]37年（[[1904年]]）1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築 == 柱祭り == 1782年（天明2年）より、諏訪大社の[[御柱祭]]に倣った「柱祭り」が行われている。以来6年ごとの[[申|申年]]と[[寅|寅年]]の4月に、柱を献上する者の山から4本の[[スギ|杉]]が伐り出され、町内を練り歩いた後、宮入りして本殿四隅に建立される。 2001年（平成13年）、鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財に指定。前回の開催は2004年（平成16年）で、次回は2010年（平成22年）4月に行われる。 == 交通アクセス == [[智頭急行]]またはJR[[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]]より徒歩15分 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja, Chidzu, Tottori|諏訪神社}} * [http://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/dd.aspx?menuid=1296 柱祭り]（智頭町役場ウェブサイト） {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社|すわしんしやちつ]] [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:智頭町]]

{{神社| |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[画像:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|250px|諏訪神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地= [[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]大字智頭宮前 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} |祭神= 建御名方神 |社格= |創建= [[1278年]]（[[弘安]]元年） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= [[4月27日]]、[[10月27日]] |神事= 柱祭り（6年おき） }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]の[[智頭宿]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]の[[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）で、[[信濃国|信州]]の[[諏訪大社]]の分霊を奉るため建てられた。[[江戸時代]]は[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所として栄えた。 牛臥山の山麓に鎮座し、参道は智頭往来に接する。境内には古来より社紋である[[カエデ|楓]]が数百本繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。 == 由緒 == * [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]） - [[信濃国]][[諏訪大社]]の分霊を勧請奉祀し、「'''諏訪大明神'''」の社号で創建 * [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）12月 - [[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失 * 江戸時代 - 鳥取藩主池田家の祈願所となる * [[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]） - 柱祭りを開始 * [[天保]]3年（[[1832年]]）11月 - 本殿を再建 * [[慶応|慶應]]4年（[[1868年]]）8月 - 合祭神を期に「'''諏訪神社'''」に改号 * [[明治]]37年（[[1904年]]）1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築 == 柱祭り == 1782年（天明2年）より、諏訪大社の[[御柱祭]]に倣った「柱祭り」が行われている。以来6年ごとの[[申|申年]]と[[寅|寅年]]の4月に、柱を献上する者の山から4本の[[スギ|杉]]が伐り出され、町内を練り歩いた後、宮入りして本殿四隅に建立される。 2001年（平成13年）3月23日、鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財に指定[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument 諏訪神社の柱祭り｜鳥取県教育委員会事務局文化財課文化財係（2015年4月29日閲覧）]。6年ごとの4月に行われる。 == 交通アクセス == * [[智頭急行]]またはJR[[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]]より徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == ギャラリー == ファイル:Suwazinzya1.jpg|諏訪神社の紅葉 ファイル:Chidu suwajinja04 1920.jpg|拝殿前の楓の林 ファイル:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|石段 ファイル:Suwazinzya2.jpg|石段下の紅葉 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|諏訪神社}} * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ 諏訪神社｜鳥取県智頭町観光協会（2015年4月29日閲覧）] * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ 諏訪神社御柱祭り｜鳥取県智頭町観光協会（2015年4月29日閲覧）] * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ 諏訪神社｜智頭急行株式会社（2015年4月29日閲覧）] * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 諏訪神社｜公益社団法人 鳥取県観光連盟（2015年4月29日閲覧）] {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社|すわしんしやちつ]] [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:智頭町の歴史]]

拝殿 |所在地= [[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]大字智頭宮前 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} |祭神= 建御名方神 |社格= |創建= [[1278年]]（[[弘安]]元年） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= [[4月27日]]、[[10月27日]] |神事= 柱祭り（6年おき） }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]の[[智頭宿]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]の[[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）で、[[信濃国|信州]]の[[諏訪大社]]の分霊を奉るため建てられた。[[江戸時代]]は[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所として栄えた。 牛臥山の山麓に鎮座し、参道は智頭往来に接する。境内には古より社紋である[[カエデ|楓]]が数百本繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。 == 由緒 == * [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]） - [[信濃国]][[諏訪大社]]の分霊を勧請奉祀し、「'''諏訪大明神'''」の社号で創建 * [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）12月 - [[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失 * 江戸時代 - 鳥取藩主池田家の祈願所となる * [[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]） - 柱祭りを開始 * [[天保]]3年（[[1832年]]）11月 - 本殿を再建 * [[慶応|慶應]]4年（[[1868年]]）8月 - 合祭神を期に「'''諏訪神社'''」に改号 * [[明治]]37年（[[1904年]]）1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築 == 境内 == *本殿 - [[江戸時代]][[天保]]3年（1832年）再建 *幣拝殿 - [[明治]]37年（1904年）再建 == 柱祭り == 1782年（天明2年）より、諏訪大社の[[御柱祭]]に倣った「柱祭り」が行われている。以来6年ごとの[[申|申年]]と[[寅|寅年]]の4月に、柱を献上する者の山から4本の[[スギ|杉]]が伐り出され、町内を練り歩いた後、宮入りして本殿四隅に建立される。 2001年（平成13年）3月23日、鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財に指定[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument 諏訪神社の柱祭り｜鳥取県教育委員会事務局文化財課文化財係（2015年4月29日閲覧）]。 == 交通アクセス == * [[智頭急行]]またはJR[[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]]より徒歩15分 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|石段 ファイル:Suwazinzya2.jpg|石段下の紅葉 ファイル:Suwazinzya1.jpg|紅葉 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|諏訪神社}} * [[鳥取県指定文化財一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ 諏訪神社｜鳥取県智頭町観光協会（2015年4月29日閲覧）] * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ 諏訪神社御柱祭り｜鳥取県智頭町観光協会（2015年4月29日閲覧）] * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ 諏訪神社｜智頭急行株式会社（2015年4月29日閲覧）] * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 諏訪神社｜公益社団法人 鳥取県観光連盟（2015年4月29日閲覧）] {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社|すわしんしやちつ]] [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:智頭町の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[画像:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|諏訪神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地= [[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]大字智頭宮前 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} |祭神= 建御名方神 |社格= |創建= [[1278年]]（[[弘安]]元年） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= [[4月27日]]、[[10月27日]] |神事= 柱祭り（6年おき） }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]の[[弘安]]元年（1278年）であり、[[信濃国|信州]]の[[諏訪大社]]の分霊を奉るため建てられた。[[江戸時代]]は[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所として栄えた。 牛臥山の山麓に鎮座し、参道は智頭往来に接する。境内には古より社紋である[[カエデ|楓]]が数百本繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。 == 由緒 == * [[弘安]]元年（1278年） - [[信濃国]][[諏訪大社]]の分霊を勧請奉祀し、'''諏訪大明神'''の社号で創建。 * [[天正]]11年（1583年）12月 - [[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失。 * 江戸時代 - [[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所となる。 * [[天明]]2年（1782年） - 柱祭りを開始。 * [[天保]]3年（1832年）11月 - 本殿を再建。 * [[慶応|慶應]]4年（1868年）8月 - 合祭神を期に'''諏訪神社'''に改号。 * 1904年（明治37年）1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築。 == 境内 == *本殿 - [[江戸時代]]の[[天保]]3年（1832年）再建 *幣拝殿 - [[明治]]37年（1904年）再建 == 柱祭り == 天明2年（1782年）より、[[信濃国]]の[[諏訪大社]][[御柱祭]]に倣った「柱祭り」が行われている。以来6年ごとの[[申|申年]]と[[寅|寅年]]の4月に、柱を献上する者の山から4本の[[スギ|杉]]が伐り出され、町内を練り歩いた後、宮入りして本殿四隅に建立される。 2001年（平成13年）3月23日、諏訪神社の柱祭りが鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財に指定された[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument 諏訪神社の柱祭り] 鳥取県教育委員会、2015年4月29日閲覧[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ 智頭町の指定・登録文化財] 智頭町。 == 文化財 == === 鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財 === * 諏訪神社の柱祭り - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定。 === 智頭町保護文化財 === * 智頭宿全図（古文書） - 1987年（昭和62年）11月25日指定。 == 関連人物 == * [[国米泰石]] - 仏師。母は諏訪神社の神主の娘。 == 交通アクセス == * [[智頭急行]]またはJR[[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]]より徒歩15分 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|石段 ファイル:Suwazinzya2.jpg|石段下の紅葉 ファイル:Suwazinzya1.jpg|紅葉 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|諏訪神社}} * [[鳥取県指定文化財一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ 諏訪神社] 智頭町観光協会、2015年4月29日閲覧 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ 諏訪神社御柱祭り] 智頭町観光協会、2015年4月29日閲覧 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ 諏訪神社] 智頭急行、2015年4月29日閲覧 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 諏訪神社] 鳥取県観光連盟、2015年4月29日閲覧 {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社|すわしんしや ちつ]] [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:智頭町の歴史]] [[Category:智頭町の建築物]]

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|Festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} ==Overview== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Suwa Shrine | Image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | Location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} | Festival Deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | Established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | Main Hall = | Alternate Names = | Annual Festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | Ceremonies = Hashira Festival (every 6 years) }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira Festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **Main Hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira Festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira Festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira Festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **Map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:D]] [[Category:Dd]] [[Category:Ddd]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|Festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} ==Overview== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Suwa Shrine | Image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]

The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira Festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **Main Hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira Festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira Festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira Festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **Map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:D]] [[Category:Ddd]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira Festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **Main Hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira Festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira Festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira Festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **Map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|Festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} ==Overview== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Suwa Shrine | Image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | Location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} | Festival Deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | Established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | Main Hall = | Alternate Names = | Annual Festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | Ceremonies = Hashira Festival (every 6 years) }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira Festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **Main Hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira Festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira Festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira Festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **Map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira Festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **Main Hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira Festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira Festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira Festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira Festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **Map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira Festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} ==Overview== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} | festival deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | annual festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]]

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia23]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia38]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia38]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia38]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia38]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia38]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia38]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia23]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]

The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor.
