Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}}

On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}}

Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[画像:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|諏訪神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地= [[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]大字智頭宮前 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|scale:30000}} |祭神= 建御名方神 |社格= |創建= [[1278年]]（[[弘安]]元年） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= [[4月27日]]、[[10月27日]] |神事= 柱祭り（6年おき） }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[鳥取県]][[八頭郡]][[智頭町]]にある[[神社]]。創建は[[鎌倉時代]]の[[弘安]]元年（1278年）であり、[[信濃国|信州]]の[[諏訪大社]]の分霊を奉るため建てられた。[[江戸時代]]は[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所として栄えた。 牛臥山の山麓に鎮座し、参道は智頭往来に接する。境内には古より社紋である[[カエデ|楓]]が数百本繁茂しており、秋は[[紅葉]]の名所として賑わう。 == 由緒 == * [[弘安]]元年（1278年） - [[信濃国]][[諏訪大社]]の分霊を勧請奉祀し、'''諏訪大明神'''の社号で創建。 * [[天正]]11年（1583年）12月 - [[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]の淀山攻略の兵火を受けて焼失。 * 江戸時代 - [[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏|池田家]]の祈願所となる。 * [[天明]]2年（1782年） - 柱祭りを開始。 * [[天保]]3年（1832年）11月 - 本殿を再建。 * [[慶応|慶應]]4年（1868年）8月 - 合祭神を期に'''諏訪神社'''に改号。 * 1904年（明治37年）1月 - 幣殿・拝殿・釣殿を改築。 == 境内 == *本殿 - [[江戸時代]]の[[天保]]3年（1832年）再建 *幣拝殿 - [[明治]]37年（1904年）再建 == 柱祭り == 天明2年（1782年）より、[[信濃国]]の[[諏訪大社]][[御柱祭]]に倣った「柱祭り」が行われている。以来6年ごとの[[申|申年]]と[[寅|寅年]]の4月に、柱を献上する者の山から4本の[[スギ|杉]]が伐り出され、町内を練り歩いた後、宮入りして本殿四隅に建立される。 2001年（平成13年）3月23日、諏訪神社の柱祭りが鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財に指定された[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument 諏訪神社の柱祭り] 鳥取県教育委員会、2015年4月29日閲覧[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ 智頭町の指定・登録文化財] 智頭町。 == 文化財 == === 鳥取県指定無形民俗文化財 === * 諏訪神社の柱祭り - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定。 === 智頭町保護文化財 === * 智頭宿全図（古文書） - 1987年（昭和62年）11月25日指定。 == 関連人物 == * [[国米泰石]] - 仏師。母は諏訪神社の神主の娘。 == 交通アクセス == * [[智頭急行]]またはJR[[因美線]]、[[智頭駅]]より徒歩15分 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|石段 ファイル:Suwazinzya2.jpg|石段下の紅葉 ファイル:Suwazinzya1.jpg|紅葉 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|諏訪神社}} * [[鳥取県指定文化財一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ 諏訪神社] 智頭町観光協会、2015年4月29日閲覧 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ 諏訪神社御柱祭り] 智頭町観光協会、2015年4月29日閲覧 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ 諏訪神社] 智頭急行、2015年4月29日閲覧 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 諏訪神社] 鳥取県観光連盟、2015年4月29日閲覧 {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社|すわしんしや ちつ]] [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:智頭町の歴史]] [[Category:智頭町の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] to [[Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (智頭町)|version=98686262|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|16|N|134|13|53|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg|300px|Suwa Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[Yazu District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | established = [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[April 27]], [[October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The Onbashira [[Matsuri|festival]], held in the [[Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}} The shrine was founded in [[1278]] (1st year of [[Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]]. During the [[Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season. == History == * [[1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[Tottori Domain]]. * [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed. == Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[1904]] (37th year of Meiji) == Hashira festival == Since [[1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa Taisha]] in [[Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall. On March 23, [[2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015. == Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei) === Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa) == Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (智頭町)|version=98686262|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]]

His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[Chizu Station]] on the [[Chizu Express]] or JR [[Inbi Line]] == Gallery == File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]] == External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ]] ]] ]] ]] [[ja]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (智頭町)|version=98686262|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chizu, Tottori]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]]
