revision.comment: Bot: categorize pages with unclosed brackets
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[fr:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[fr:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[fr:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3505843]] Wikidata name: Suwa Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in [[:en:Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.2711|N|134.231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[:en:Yazu District|Yazu District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | established = [[:en:1278|1278]] (1st year of [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = [[:en:April 27|April 27]], [[:en:October 27|October 27]] | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) | location = {{ill|Chizu|WD=Q1156212}} }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Chizu, Tottori|Chizu]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Known from the time of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions]], the shrine is celebrated for its [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] and [[:en:Momiji|Momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher=[[:en:Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref> The Onbashira [[Japanese festivals|festival]], held in the [[:en:Tiger (zodiac)|Year of the Tiger]] and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Year of the Rooster]], sees pillars of [[:en:Japanese cedar|Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher=[[:en:Chizu, Tottori|Chizu Town]] |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref>
The shrine was founded in [[:en:1278|1278]] (1st year of [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]]) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it flourished as a prayer site for the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]].
Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season.
== History == * [[:en:1278|1278]] (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * [[:en:1583|1583]] (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]], the lords of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]]. * [[:en:1782|1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * [[:en:1832|1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * [[:en:1868|1868]] (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * [[:en:1904|1904]] (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in [[:en:1832|1832]] (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in [[:en:1904|1904]] (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since [[:en:1782|1782]] (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, [[:en:2001|2001]] (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Chizu Station|Chizu Station]] on the [[:en:Chizu Express|Chizu Express]] or JR [[:en:Inbi Line|Inbi Line]]
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]]
== External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[:en:Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)|Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]
==References== {{Reflist}}]][[:en:Ja|Ja]]

Known from the time of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions}}, the shrine is celebrated for its {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} and [[:en:Momiji|Momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref> The Onbashira [[Japanese festivals|festival]], held in the {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|en|Tiger (zodiac)|WD=Q740762|lt=Year of the Tiger}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=Year of the Rooster}}, sees pillars of [[:en:Japanese cedar|Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref>
The shrine was founded in {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan}}) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it flourished as a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain}}.
Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season.
== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line}}
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]]
== External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[:en:Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)|Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]

Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season.
== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line}}
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[fr:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[fr:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[en:Suwa Shrine (Tottori)]][[fr:Suwa-jinja (Chizu)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (智頭町)]]
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in {{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu}} | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.2711|N|134.231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[:en:Yazu District|Yazu District]], {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | established = {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan}}) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = {{ill|April 27|en|April 27|WD=Q2533|lt=April 27}}, {{ill|October 27|en|October 27|WD=Q2956|lt=October 27}} | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) | location = {{ill|Chizu|WD=Q1156212}} }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Known from the time of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions}}, the shrine is celebrated for its {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} and [[:en:Momiji|Momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref> The Onbashira [[Japanese festivals|festival]], held in the {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|en|Tiger (zodiac)|WD=Q740762|lt=Year of the Tiger}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=Year of the Rooster}}, sees pillars of [[:en:Japanese cedar|Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref>
The shrine was founded in {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan}}) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it flourished as a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain}}.
Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season.
== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line}}
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]]
== External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[:en:Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)|Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]
==References== {{Reflist}}]][[:en:Ja|Ja]]

During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it flourished as a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}.

== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583|1=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station|1=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express|1=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}}
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in {{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu|1=Chizu, Tottori}} | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.2711|N|134.231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[:en:Yazu District|Yazu District]], {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | established = {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = {{ill|April 27|en|April 27|WD=Q2533|lt=April 27|1=April 27}}, {{ill|October 27|en|October 27|WD=Q2956|lt=October 27|1=October 27}} | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) | location = {{ill|Chizu|WD=Q1156212|1=Chizu, Tottori}} }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu|1=Chizu, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Known from the time of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}, the shrine is celebrated for its {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova|1=Zelkova serrata}} and [[:en:Momiji|Momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town|1=Chizu, Tottori}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref> The Onbashira [[Japanese festivals|festival]], held in the {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|en|Tiger (zodiac)|WD=Q740762|lt=Year of the Tiger|1=Tiger (zodiac)}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=Year of the Rooster|1=Rooster (zodiac)}}, sees pillars of [[:en:Japanese cedar|Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town|1=Chizu, Tottori}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref>
The shrine was founded in {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it flourished as a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}.
Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season.
== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583|1=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station|1=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express|1=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}}
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]]
== External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[:en:Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)|Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]

During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it flourished as a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}.

== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583|1=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station|1=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express|1=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}}

Known from the time of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}, the shrine is celebrated for its {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova|1=Zelkova serrata}} and [[:en:Momiji|Momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town|1=Chizu, Tottori}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref> The Onbashira [[Japanese festivals|festival]], held in the {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|en|Tiger (zodiac)|WD=Q740762|lt=Year of the Tiger|1=Tiger (zodiac)}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=Year of the Rooster|1=Rooster (zodiac)}}, sees pillars of [[:en:Japanese cedar|Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town|1=Chizu, Tottori}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref>
The shrine was founded in {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it flourished as a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}.
Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season.
== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583|1=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station|1=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express|1=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}}
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)|Suwa Shrine}} * [[List of Cultural Properties of Tottori Prefecture]]
== External Links == * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Chizu Town Tourism Association, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://site5.tori-info.co.jp/p/chizukyu/guide/chizu/suwajinjya/ Suwa Shrine] Chizu Express, accessed April 29, 2015 * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/448 Suwa Shrine] Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, accessed April 29, 2015 ==See also== {{Commons Category|Suwa-jinja (Chizu)}} * [[:en:Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)|Suwa Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} | image = File:Chidu suwajinja01 1920.jpg | caption = Suwa Jinja in {{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu|1=Chizu, Tottori}} | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.2711|N|134.231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Suwa Shrine | location = Miyamae, Chizu, [[Chizu Town]], [[:en:Yazu District|Yazu District]], {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} | deity = Takeminakata-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = | established = {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}) | main hall = | alternate names = | festival = {{ill|April 27|en|April 27|WD=Q2533|lt=April 27|1=April 27}}, {{ill|October 27|en|October 27|WD=Q2956|lt=October 27|1=October 27}} | ceremony = Hashira festival (every 6 years) | location = {{ill|Chizu|WD=Q1156212|1=Chizu, Tottori}} }} {{nihongo|'''Suwa Jinja'''|諏訪神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu|1=Chizu, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Known from the time of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}, the shrine is celebrated for its {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova|1=Zelkova serrata}} and [[:en:Momiji|Momiji]]. The [[Honden]] dates to 1832.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/suwa/ |title=Suwa Jinja |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town|1=Chizu, Tottori}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref> The Onbashira [[Japanese festivals|festival]], held in the {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|en|Tiger (zodiac)|WD=Q740762|lt=Year of the Tiger|1=Tiger (zodiac)}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=Year of the Rooster|1=Rooster (zodiac)}}, sees pillars of [[:en:Japanese cedar|Japanese cedar]] carried through the town.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://cms.sanin.jp/p/chizu/kankou/rekishi_miru/hashira/ |title=Suwa Jinja Onbashira |publisher={{ill|Chizu, Tottori|en|Chizu, Tottori|WD=Q1156212|lt=Chizu Town|1=Chizu, Tottori}} |accessdate=23 April 2011}}</ref>
The shrine was founded in {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}) and was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it flourished as a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}.
Situated at the base of Mount Ushigase, the approach to the shrine connects with the Chizu Highway. The precincts are home to hundreds of maple trees, the shrine's emblem, which attract many visitors during the autumn foliage season.
== History == * {{ill|1278|en|1278|WD=Q5533|lt=1278|1=1278}} (1st year of Kōan) - The shrine was established to enshrine a branch spirit from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} under the name "Suwa Daimyōjin." * {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583|1=1583}} (11th year of Tenshō), December - The shrine was burned down during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Mount Yodo. * Edo period - It became a prayer site for the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}, the lords of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}. * {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei) - The Hashira festival began. * {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō), November - The main hall was rebuilt. * {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (4th year of Keiō), August - The shrine was renamed "Suwa Shrine" following the addition of new deities. * {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji), January - The Heiden, Haiden, and Tsuri-den were reconstructed.
== Grounds == * **main hall** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}} (3rd year of Tenpō) * **Heiden and Haiden** - Rebuilt in {{ill|1904|en|1904|WD=Q2046|lt=1904|1=1904}} (37th year of Meiji)
== Hashira festival == Since {{ill|1782|en|1782|WD=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}} (2nd year of Tenmei), the "Hashira festival," modeled after the [[Onbashira festival]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, has been held every six years in the years of the Monkey and Tiger in the lunar calendar. During the festival, four cedar logs are cut from the designated mountains, paraded through the town, and erected at the four corners of the main hall.
On March 23, {{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (13th year of Heisei), the Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Tottori Prefecture<ref>[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/FD8385FB128EC7254925796F0007FF81?OpenDocument Suwa Shrine Hashira festival] Tottori Prefecture Education Committee, accessed April 29, 2015</ref><ref>[https://www1.town.chizu.tottori.jp/chizu/kyouiku/27/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chizu Town] Chizu Town, accessed April 29, 2015</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == === Tottori Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * **Hashira festival of Suwa Shrine** - Designated on March 23, 2001 (13th year of Heisei)
=== Chizu Town Protected Cultural Property === * **map of Chizu Inn** (Ancient Document) - Designated on November 25, 1987 (62nd year of Shōwa)
== Notable Individuals == * **Kokubei Taishi** - A sculptor. His mother was the daughter of a Suwa Shrine priest.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chizu Station|en|Chizu Station|WD=Q5102669|lt=Chizu Station|1=Chizu Station}} on the {{ill|Chizu Express|en|Chizu Express|WD=Q4133832|lt=Chizu Express|1=Chizu Express}} or JR {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}}
== Gallery == <gallery widths="110px" heights="165px"> File:Chidu suwajinja02 2048.jpg|Stone steps File:Suwazinzya2.jpg|Autumn foliage at the base of the stone steps File:Suwazinzya1.jpg|Autumn foliage </gallery>
